issues_found: H3 (layer as temperature) is FALSIFIED but NOT honestly reported in the paper. The full_cross_layer_critical experiment shows absorption_rate=1.0 for ALL layers (0, 3, 6, 9, 11) at lambda=0.001, directly contradicting the 'layer 6 at critical point' narrative. The paper conflates this with probe-based layer pattern analysis - these are fundamentally different experiments measuring different phenomena.

The cross-layer experiment at critical sparsity (lambda_c=5e-5) was planned in the proposal but NOT executed. The executed experiment used lambda=0.001, which is 20x higher than the proposed critical sparsity. The paper cannot claim to have tested layer-dependence at criticality without the right sparsity level.

Multiple comparisons still uncorrected. At least 12 hypothesis tests performed. Layer 8 correlation (p=0.023) does NOT survive Bonferroni correction (adjusted alpha=0.0042). Only pairwise Fisher z-tests survive (8 vs 5: p=0.0036, 8 vs 10: p=0.0011). This was flagged in iter 3 and remains unfixed.

chi_ratio=1.88 is below the 'sharp transition' threshold of 3.0. The proposal correctly uses 'quasi-critical' but the integrated paper still uses 'phase transition' language throughout. A physics-savvy reviewer will flag this as overstated framing.

H4 direction is REVERSED but framed as 'discovery' without acknowledging falsification. Original H4 predicted CV_low < CV_high; actual result shows CV_high >> CV_low by factor 6-7x. This is a directional falsification (t>1000, p≈0), not just a discovery.

H2 framing still contains HARKing despite being flagged in iter 3 critique. 'layer 8 achieves rho=0.705 > 0.6, satisfying the threshold' frames post-hoc observation as meeting pre-registered threshold. Original H2 threshold was for mean across layers, not cherry-picked layer.

'Training-invariant' overclaim persists in subtle form. Section 6.1 states 'decoder normalization emerges from training dynamics rather than architectural design alone.' Causal claim unsupported - no training dynamics were analyzed.

CV-steering connection is correlational, not causal. pilot_steering_cv shows high-CV features have 2x steering effect (0.153 vs 0.075) but pilot-stage (n=30), not full validation. Why high-CV features are more steerable is unexplained.
Lambda_c shows 10x instability across SAE configurations. Prospective validation not executed. Value lambda_c=5e-5 could be artifact of specific SAE/dataset combination.
Nu=3 scaling exponent derived from only 3 dictionary sizes (6144, 12288, 24576). Single estimate not confirmed. Needs independent replication on held-out dictionary sizes (8k, 32k).
